introSection: Boardwalk Bullet is a wooden roller coaster located at Kemah Boardwalk in Kemah, Texas, USA. It was designed by The Gravity Group, LLC and manufactured by Martin & Vleminckx.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Boardwalk Bullet was announced in December 2006. It opened on August 31, 2007.

Kemah Boardwalk was hit by Hurricane Ike on September 15, 2008. While Boardwalk Bullet itself was not significantly damaged, it remained closed until November of that year while the amusement park was rebuilt.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Maximum track banking of 75 degrees.

There are a total of forty-two crossovers throughout the course of the track.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were overhauled by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company in 2017.

fullText:
Boardwalk Bullet is a wooden roller coaster located at Kemah Boardwalk in Kemah, Texas, USA. It was designed by The Gravity Group, LLC and manufactured by Martin & Vleminckx.

Boardwalk Bullet was announced in December 2006. It opened on August 31, 2007.

Kemah Boardwalk was hit by Hurricane Ike on September 15, 2008. While Boardwalk Bullet itself was not significantly damaged, it remained closed until November of that year while the amusement park was rebuilt.

Maximum track banking of 75 degrees.

There are a total of forty-two crossovers throughout the course of the track.

The trains were overhauled by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company in 2017.
